title: The Foundation of Exploratory Testing
excerpt: Discover why intuition remains the most powerful tool in a manual tester's arsenal, despite the rise of automated scripts.
author: The Manual Authority
content:
Exploratory testing is more than just 'clicking around'. It is a structured approach to software testing that emphasizes the personal freedom and responsibility of the individual tester to continually optimize the quality of their work by treating test-related learning, test design, test execution, and test result interpretation as mutually supportive activities that run in parallel throughout the project.

Key Principles

  • Simultaneous learning, test design, and execution.
  • The tester's intuition and experience are central.
  • Adaptability to new information discovered during testing.
